title: Autoencoders as learned compression
key_concepts: Autoencoders are neural networks trained to reconstruct their own input.
By passing data through a bottleneck layer (lower dimension than input), the network is forced to learn a compressed, latent representation.
This represents a shift to the 'dipelajari mesin' (learned by machine) side of the spectrum. However, it still learns specifically from the dataset it is trained on, not external data.
equations: Objective is to minimize reconstruction loss: $\mathcal{L}(X, \hat{X}) = \|X - g_{\phi}(f_{\theta}(X))\|^2$
callouts: Spectrum callout: Explicitly state this is an example of 'representasi yang dipelajari mesin' (learned by machine) because the network weights discover the features, unlike PCA's strict mathematical formulation.
examples: Using a deep autoencoder to compress noisy sensor data into a clean 10-dimensional vector before feeding it to a random forest.
